title: reseda undata var. baetica
intro: Reseda undata var. baetica, also known as Baetic Reseda or Spanish Valerian, is a plant species belonging to the Borage family (Boraginaceae), native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its essential oil composition. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting better sleep. Reseda undata var. baetica can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, which are commonly used in herbal remedies and traditional medicine.
